rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend The Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 4, 2018, Line 1, ASSIGNEE NDS, LLC Motion To Amend The Judgment Assignee and Judgment Creditor NDS, LLC's motion to amend the judgment is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and an a proposed amended judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH HALE ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 4, 2018, Line 3, RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM'S Motion To Change Venue Respondent California Public Employees' Retirement System's motion to change venue is denied. Petitioner properly venued this action in San Francisco per CCP section 401(1) because the action could have been filed in Sacramento and the Attorney General has an office in San Francisco. Respondent fails to show that "the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice" warrant a transfer to Sacramento. (CCP §397(c) (emphasis added).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for petitioner is required to prepare a proposed which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Martin Eng, Lombard Flats, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Rescind Sale Of Defendant Martin Engs Ownership Interest In Lombard Flats, Llc By Braunco And Plaintiff Legal Recovery, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 4, 2018, Line 19, Martin Eng, Lombard Flats, LLC'S Motion To Rescind Sale Of Defendant Martin Engs Ownership Interest In Lombard Flats, Llc By Braunco And Plaintiff Legal Recovery, Llc Defendant Martin Eng's motion to rescind sale of his ownership interest in Lombard Flats, LLC is denied. CCP section 701.680, by its terms, applies to all sales of property "pursuant to" article 6 of chapter 3 of the Enforcement of Judgments Act. Article 6 covers all post-judgment sales except those that are specifically exempted from the coverage of article 6, which includes all sales of LLC interests per charging orders. Mr. Eng's argument that his discovery of the sale was delayed and is not time-barred by section 701.680(c)(1) is not supported by any evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Legal Recovery is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TYLER JOHNSTON VS. SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 4, 2018, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT CENTRAL STRIPING SERVICE, INC. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendant Central Striping Service, Inc.'s demurrer to all five causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by Synergy Project Management is overruled as to all five cause of action. The argument that the cross-claims must be arbitrated needs to be raised by a motion to compel arbitration because of the possible application of CCP 1281.2(c) which requires evidence by the parties bearing on the exercise of the court's discretion. All claims are adequately alleged. However, the court's own motion per CCP 128.7 is granted as to the third and fourth causes of action because, as Synergy acknowledges, it can only state a claim for express indemnity and is not able to state a claim for implied indemnity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Central Striping is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAMIRO HERNANDEZ ET AL VS. SKANSKA USA BUILDING, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 4, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANTS SKANSKA USA BUILDING, INC., WEBCOR / OBAYASHI JOINT VENTURE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Skanska USA Building, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Skanska fails to maintain its initial burden of production per CCP section 437c. The Privette doctrine does not apply to a non-hirer subcontractor such as Skanska in this case. The doctrine is based on notions of fairness. "[A]n independent contractor's employee should not be allowed to recover damages from the contractor's hirer, who 'is indirectly paying for the cost of [workers' compensation] coverage, which the [hired] contractor presumably has calculated into the contract price.'" (Tverberg v. Fillner Construction, Inc. (2010) 49 Cal.4th 518, 525.) No such fairness issue exists with regard to a non-hirer such as Skanska. Alvarez v. Seaside Transportation Services LLC (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 635 does not extend the Privette doctrine to all subcontractors on a worksite. The case does not expressly address that issue and it appears that the subcontractors in that case obtained summary judgment because they were being sued as agents of the hirer. (Alvarez, 13 Cal.App.5th at 644, fn.4.) In this case, plaintiffs are suing Skanska for its direct negligence. Paragraphs 17-21 of the complaint allege that Skanska breached its duty to maintain and control the area where the accident occurred. Skanska does not present evidence showing that (1) it met that duty and (2) plaintiffs' claim cannot be established. As an additional ground to deny the motion, even if Skanska had satisfied its initial summary motion burden, plaintiffs created a triable dispute by presenting evidence showing that Skanska did in fact breach its duty. That evidence included Skanska had control of the zone where the injury occurred and a responsibility to put warning barriers up. (Ex. 4, Spillane Depo., 23:13-24:1; 39:19-25; 59:11-15; 89:23-90:21 (Webcor's PMK); Ex. 6 Burke Depo., 45:5-7; 53:12-17; 64:21-23; 66:24-67:19; 69:8-16 (Webcor's Construction Manager).) There is also evidence that Skanska had a responsibility to abate hazards within its controlled zone. (Ex. 10, Buckignani Depo., 110:6-14 (Webcor Senior Safety Manager).) Skanska does not establish that it delineated the zone or abated the hazards. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAMIRO HERNANDEZ ET AL VS. SKANSKA USA BUILDING, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Webcor Obayashi Joint Ventures
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 4, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT SKANSKA USA BUILDING, INC., WEBCOR / OBAYASHI JOINT VENTURE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Webcor/Obayashi Joint Venture's motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable dispute whether WOJV negligently retained control of the worksite and affirmatively contributed to Mr. Hernandez's injury. Plaintiffs presented evidence showing that WOJV retained control over the worksite (Ex. 6, Burke Depo., 54:20-25; 9:5-12) and made the decision to send Anvil into the injury zone even though the zone had yet to be made safe by Skanska or properly turned over to WOJV. (Ex. 4, Spillane Depo., 30:2-12; Ex. 5, Garcia Depo., 30:1-7.) "While the passive permitting of an unsafe condition to occur is not an affirmative contribution, the act of directing that it occur is active participation." (Tverberg v. Fillner Construction (2012) 202 CalApp.4th 1439, 1448.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GILBERT PAPAZIAN II VS. KEVIN MICHAEL SULLIVAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Furthre Responses To Judicial Counsil Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 4, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANTs GRIFFIN & SULLIVAN and KEVIN SULLIVAN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Judicial Council Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER SARIQUE EVANGELISTA ET AL VS. ROLAND GENE DALE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-18-568172, CGC-18-568278 WITH CASE CGC-18-567976
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 4, 2018, Line 16, DEFENDANT GENE ROLAND'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-18-568172, CGC-18-568278 WITH CASE CGC-18-567976 Defendant Gene Roland's motion to consolidate case 567976 with cases 568172 and 568278 is granted. No opposition filed. The three cases are consolidated for all purposes. The lead case is case 567976 and all further papers in all three cases must now be filed only in the lead case. The case management conference set for January 16, 2019 in 567976 now applies to all three cases. The case management conferences previously set for December 19, 2018 and December 26, 2018 in 568172 and 568278 are now vacated. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Roland is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
